title: Contact
text:
This dream is different.

The clearing is gone. You are in a space that is defined by warmth and the particular quality of firelight that has been burning low for hours. She is there, and she is closer than she has been, and this time the dream has provided detail — the shape of her hands, the way she holds her weight, the precise quality of her attention.

She touches your face.

The dream is very clear about this. Her touch is not aggressive or urgent. It is the touch of someone establishing a fact — something like: this is the shape of what you're becoming. Something like: I made this and I recognize it.

Your body's response is involuntary and loud and wrong in the way that wrongness has been slowly reclassifying itself since Stage One.

You try to say something. The dream doesn't let you. It is not interested in what you have to say about this. It is only interested in what your body says, which is considerably less ambiguous.

You wake with arousal that doesn't fade quickly. You note that this is the third dream. You note that they are escalating. You note both of these things instead of noting the other thing, which is that you were not entirely glad to wake up.
